problem_statement:
Go completion broken when MarkPersistentRequiredFlag() called on root command

While working on supporting required flags for Go completions (Fish and Zsh v2) I hit the following bug.

If a persistent flag on the root command is marked as required, e.g., 
```
rootCmd.PersistentFlags().String("flag", "", "required persistent")
rootCmd.MarkPersistentFlagRequired("flag")
```
it breaks the `__complete` command.

This is because the `__complete` command, being a child of the root command, inherits the required persistent flag, and since that flag is not specified when calling `__complete`, the `__complete` command fails to execute.
```
$ ./testprog __complete ""
Error: required flag(s) "flag" not set
```
I think it is and unlikely case though; if the root command would have a required persistent flag, it would imply that that flag would need to be specified in every single case of calling the program; I have trouble imagining a use case for that.

Still, it should probably be fixed.
I don't have a solution right now, but I will be investigating if defining a `PreRun` on `__complete`  can allow us to turn off required flags for `__complete`.

In the unlikely case of this bug, the impacts are:
- bash: Go custom completions (`ValidArgsFunction` and `RegisterFlagCompletionFunc()`) would not work (but everything else would)
- fish: all completions would not work
